steps.name: Noise Reduction
steps.summary: Fast non-local means denoising to stabilize packaging surfaces and small text regions.

steps.name: Contrast Enhancement
steps.summary: CLAHE-based contrast enhancement to make packaging structure and printed text more distinct.

steps.name: Binarization
steps.summary: Adaptive thresholding used internally to support OCR-friendly text-region analysis.

steps.name: Detection
steps.summary: 1 object(s) detected with annotated bounding boxes.

steps.name: OCR and Result Rendering
steps.summary: The system extracts text, highlights the recognized text region, and renders the predicted label on the full image.

model_comparison.summary: Baseline quantum kernel SVM with linear ZZ entanglement.
model_comparison.strengths: Simple quantum-kernel benchmark and easy presentation baseline.
model_comparison.limitations: Often weaker than strong classical baselines on rich image features.

model_comparison.summary: Compact neural baseline over ROI feature vectors.
model_comparison.strengths: Can model nonlinearities without quantum overhead.
model_comparison.limitations: Still depends entirely on classical feature engineering.

model_comparison.summary: Linear classical baseline for a clean performance floor.
model_comparison.strengths: Fast to train and easy to explain to non-technical audiences.
model_comparison.limitations: Limited nonlinear capacity compared with richer baselines.

model_comparison.summary: Strong classical large-margin baseline on engineered ROI features.
model_comparison.strengths: Usually very strong on compact, well-separated feature spaces.
model_comparison.limitations: No quantum component, so it serves as the bar to beat.

model_comparison.summary: Tree-ensemble baseline using the same classical ROI features.
model_comparison.strengths: Robust and interpretable feature-importance style baseline.
model_comparison.limitations: Can plateau on subtle visual differences compared with SVM/MLP.

model_comparison.summary: Quantum kernel SVM using a Pauli feature map with mixed operators.
model_comparison.strengths: More expressive than plain ZZ kernels for research comparison.
model_comparison.limitations: More expensive and not always better on compact ROI datasets.

model_comparison.summary: Baseline quantum kernel SVM with linear ZZ entanglement.
model_comparison.strengths: Simple quantum-kernel benchmark and easy presentation baseline.
model_comparison.limitations: Often weaker than strong classical baselines on rich image features.

model_comparison.summary: Quantum kernel SVM with denser ZZ entanglement across qubits.
model_comparison.strengths: Tests whether richer entanglement improves separability.
model_comparison.limitations: Higher circuit complexity and slower similarity evaluation.

model_comparison.summary: Variational quantum classifier with RealAmplitudes ansatz.
model_comparison.strengths: Reasonable first trainable quantum classifier for presentations.
model_comparison.limitations: Optimization noise and local minima can reduce consistency.

model_comparison.summary: Variational quantum classifier with an EfficientSU2 ansatz.
model_comparison.strengths: Higher expressivity for studying trainable quantum circuits.
model_comparison.limitations: Usually slower and more complex than simpler VQC variants.
